title: To amend the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act to expand eligibility for certain wastewater infrastructure grants, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules about who can get money from the government to help with wastewater and clean water projects. Right now, only local governments and Indian tribes can apply for these grants. The bill would add state governments to the list so they can also apply for this money. This means more groups can get help to improve their water systems and keep water clean.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Water Resources and Environment.

sections.title: Section 1  Wastewater Infrastructure and Trash-Free Waters Grants
sections.text: This section changes a law called the Save Our Seas 2.0 Act. The change adds state governments to the list of groups that can receive grants for wastewater infrastructure and trash-free waters projects. Before this change, only local governments like cities and counties, and Indian tribes could apply for these grants. Now state governments can also apply for this federal funding to improve wastewater systems and keep waters free from trash.
